title: DNA Barcoding Protocol
author: Jordan Beasley
materials: Aliquot 13.6µl PCR mastermix into each well.

steps.title: Spin down the sample plate and...
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample plate and inspect to check no tissue/sample is stuck in the lids of the sealing caps.

steps.title: Remove the caps and remove the ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove the caps and remove the ethanol. Use a single channel pipette for very small samples and take care to ensure the sample is not aspirated into the tip and removed with the ethanol.

steps.title: Place the plate on a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on a thermocycler with no lid or seal and with the thermocycler lid open to allow the remaining ethanol to evaporate. Set the thermocycler to run at 70 ⁰C for 10 minutes or longer if required until remaining ethanol has evaporated off. Take care when moving the plate onto and off of the thermocycler without lids- static can cause dry samples to pop out of the wells.

steps.title: Prepare a lysis mastermix using the...
steps.instruction: Prepare a lysis mastermix using the KAPA Express Extract kit (Table 1). Reagent Volume µl (1 rxn) 1 Molecular grade water 44 2 1U/µl KAPA Express Extract Enzyme 1 3 10X KAPA Express Extract Buffer 5 Table 1: KAPA Express Extract lysis mastermix reagents and volumes

steps.title: Aliquot 50µl lysis master mix into...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 50µl lysis master mix into each well and seal with a clear PCR seal. (Note: lysis master mix volume can be reduced for very small samples)

steps.title: Spin down the plate and incubate...
steps.instruction: Spin down the plate and incubate on a thermocycler using the following program: Temperature (°C) Time (minutes) 1 30 30 2 60 30 3 95 5 4 4 ∞ Table 2: Thermocycling regime for lysis incubation.

steps.title: Make 1:10 dilution of lysate, adding 2
steps.instruction: Make 1:10 dilution of lysate, adding 2.5µl lysate to 22.5µl PCR grade water NOTE: Lysate can be stored at -20⁰C until needed.

steps.title: Prepare the PCR mastermix using the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the PCR mastermix using the KAPA Plant PCR kit as follows: Component Volume (1 x 20µl rxn) 1 KAPA Plant PCR buffer (contains dNTPs) 10.1 2 PCR grade water 2.9 3 Polymerase 0.2 4 MgCl2 (25mM) 0.4 KAPA Plant PCR master mix volumes. (NOTE: This PCR amplifies a 750bp COI amplicon using primers containing indexes for ONT sequencing. The plant PCR kit is used because it was found result in a higher success rate for samples containing inhibitors.) Primer sequences can be found here: Nanopore Tagged Folmer Barcoding Primer Sequences.xlsx

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.6µl indexed primer mix to each well.

steps.title: Add 4
steps.instruction: Add 4.8µl 1:10 diluted lysate to each well

steps.title: Seal the plate with PCR seal...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with PCR seal and mix and spin the plate.

steps.title: Place the plate on a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on a thermocycler and run the following program: 1 cycle: 95°C for 3 mins 5 cycles: 95°C for 40s 45°C for 40s 72°C for 1 min 40 cycles: 95°C for 40s 65°C for 40s 72°C for 1 min 1 cycle: 72°C for 5 mins 4°C ∞ NOTE: Plate can be stored at -20°C until needed.

steps.title: Pour 500ml 1x TAE into a glass duran bottle
steps.instruction: Pour 500ml 1x TAE into a glass duran bottle. (This makes enough gel solution to make two large gels with 4 rows of combs with 50 teeth each to take 2 plates of samples each).

steps.title: Weigh out 5g of agarose powder...
steps.instruction: Weigh out 5g of agarose powder and add to the duran bottle.

steps.title: Close the lid tightly and mix...
steps.instruction: Close the lid tightly and mix the agarose powder into the TAE by swirling the bottle.

steps.title: Loosen the lid so that air...
steps.instruction: Loosen the lid so that air can escape the bottle (so it doesn't explode) and heat the bottle in a microwave in short bursts checking between heating until the liquid starts to boil and the agarose has gone into solution and the liquid is completely clear.

steps.title: Allow the agarose to cool so...
steps.instruction: Allow the agarose to cool so that it is still molten but not hot. Tape off the ends of a large gel tray with masking/autoclave tape and place combs in the tray.

steps.title: Pour half of the agarose (~250ml)...
steps.instruction: Pour half of the agarose (~250ml) into the tray, making sure there are no bubbles present and allow to set firm.

steps.title: When completely cooled and set, remove...
steps.instruction: When completely cooled and set, remove the plastic combs and tape, place in a tank filled with enough 1x TAE buffer to cover the gel.

steps.title: Add Gel Red to loading dye in a 1:1000 ratio
steps.instruction: Add Gel Red to loading dye in a 1:1000 ratio. Load 2µl PCR product mixed with 3µl loading dye/Gel Red mix to the gel and run alongside 1Kb hyper ladder at 80 volts for ~45 mins. Check bands of the expected size are present by visualising the gel in a UV transilluminator.

steps.title: Pool 5µl PCR product for each...
steps.instruction: Pool 5µl PCR product for each sample in your pool (taking care that no two samples in the pool have the same indexes).

steps.title: Quantify the pool using Qubit, taking...
steps.instruction: Quantify the pool using Qubit, taking 3 x 1µl replicates from the pool and take the average ng/µl from the three replicates as the concentration. Qubit_HSDNA_Protocol.pdf

steps.title: Prepare library for sequencing using the...
steps.instruction: Prepare library for sequencing using the Nanopore Ligation Sequencing kit V14 as described in the attached protocol. Flongle Library Prep Protocol (ligation sequencing).pdf The library prep takes 500ng DNA in 24.5µl volume as input. The resulting library is sequenced on a flongle (v10.4.1) for 24 hours using the super-accurate basecalling setting.

steps.title: Currently, ONTBarcoder software is used to...
steps.instruction: Currently, ONTBarcoder software is used to extract consensus barcodes from the raw nanopore data. First fastq files from the run are concatenated into one fastq file containing all of the reads from the run. This file is loaded into ONTBarcoder as input along with a demultiplexing file containing the indexing information for each sample (see manual below for details). ONTBarcoder_manual.pdf ONTBarcoder can be downloaded here: https://github.com/asrivathsan/ONTbarcoder

steps.title: The barcodes from ONTBarcoder output are...
steps.instruction: The barcodes from ONTBarcoder output are then searched in BOLD for species identification. In this pipeline, a BOLD search tool called BOLDigger is used to query multiple barcodes and return the top 20 hits in BOLD. Please see BOLDigger documentation here: https://github.com/DominikBuchner/BOLDigger
